cakkhukaraṇī.meaning: producing vision; making the eye (of wisdom) arise

cakkhusamphasso.meaning: eye-contact; contact arising at the eye
cakkhusamphasso.gram: masculine noun, nominative singular, compound (cakkhu + samphassa)
breakdown: Contact, touch; saṁ + phassa, from √phus “to touch”.

cammasannāho.meaning: leather armour; a fastening of hide
cammasannāho.gram: masculine noun, nominative singular, compound (camma + sannāha)

canaṁ.meaning: even, ever (an emphasizing suffix-particle, as in kudācanaṁ “at any time”)

caraṁ.gram: present participle of carati (√car), nominative singular

cassa.meaning: and may there be; and should also be
cassa.gram: sandhi fusion: ca + assa (optative of atthi)
breakdown: May it be; optative 3rd person singular of atthi.

cattāri purisa-yugāni aṭṭha-purisa-pugga.meaning: the four pairs of men, the eight kinds of individuals (the noble persons on the four paths and four fruits)
cattāri purisa-yugāni aṭṭha-purisa-pugga.summary: This phrase from the recollection of the Saṅgha names the noble disciples: four pairs counted by path-and-fruit, eight when counted individually.
breakdown: The eight individual persons (aṭṭha “eight” + purisa + puggala “individual”).

catubbhi.gram: cardinal numeral, instrumental/ablative plural of catu

catusaccapaṭivedhāya.meaning: for the penetration of the four truths
breakdown: For the penetration, realization; dative of paṭivedha (paṭi + √vidh “to pierce”).

catūhapāyehi.meaning: from the four states of woe; the four spheres of existence below the human realm
breakdown: States of woe, lower realms; ablative plural of apāya.

catūsu.meaning: in the four (as in catūsu apāyesu, “in the four states of misery”)
catūsu.gram: cardinal numeral, locative plural of catu

cavanatā.meaning: the state of falling away; passing away, death
cavanatā.gram: feminine abstract noun (cavana + -tā, from cavati “to fall away”)

caṅkamasuttaṁ.meaning: the Caṅkama Sutta, the discourse on walking up and down
caṅkamasuttaṁ.gram: proper noun, neuter (sutta title; caṅkama + suttaṁ)

caṅkamena.meaning: by walking up and down; with the walking meditation path
caṅkamena.gram: masculine noun, instrumental singular of caṅkama (from caṅkamati)
breakdown: Walking up and down; the walking path (intensive formation from √kam).

caṇḍālakule.meaning: in an outcaste clan; in a caṇḍāla family
caṇḍālakule.gram: neuter noun, locative singular, compound (caṇḍāla + kula)
breakdown: Outcaste; a member of the lowest social group.
In the clan, family; locative singular of kula.

cetosamathakovido.meaning: skilled in the calming of the mind; well acquainted with mental tranquillity
cetosamathakovido.gram: adjective, nominative singular masculine, compound (ceto + samatha + kovida)

cevānuyuñjanto.meaning: and also practising; and likewise applying oneself
cevānuyuñjanto.gram: sandhi phrase: ca + eva + anuyuñjanto (present participle of anuyuñjati)
breakdown: Also, just so (ca + eva fuses to ceva; eva + anu- coalesces to cevānu-).
Practising, applying oneself; present participle of anuyuñjati.

chabbaggo.gram: masculine noun, compound (cha + vagga, with sandhi chabb-)
breakdown: Six (chaḷ before a vowel; here ḷ + v assimilates to bb).
And (cha + ca fuses with doubling to chacca-).
Heinous crimes, grave offences (ca + abhi- coalesces to cābhi-).

chaddisāpaṭicchādanakaṇḍaṁ.meaning: the section on covering the six directions (in the Siṅgālovāda teaching)
breakdown: Six (with sandhi doubling of the following d).
Stupefied, paralysed by fear; past participle of chambhati.

chambhī.gram: adjective (from chambhati “to be stiff with fear”)

chamāyaṁ.meaning: on the earth; on the ground
chamāyaṁ.gram: feminine noun, locative singular of chamā

chandāgatiṁ.meaning: the wrong course of desire; going astray out of impulse or partiality
chandāgatiṁ.gram: feminine noun, accusative singular, compound (chanda + agati)
breakdown: Impulse, desire, partiality (chanda + agati coalesces to chandāgati).

chandūpasaṁhitāpi.meaning: even those connected with desire; also accompanied by wish
breakdown: Wish, desire (chanda + upa- coalesces to chandūpa-).
And, also, even (api, fused in sandhi).

channaṁ.gram: cardinal numeral, genitive plural of cha

chaḷabhiñño.gram: adjective, nominative singular masculine, compound (cha + abhiññā, with linking ḷ)
breakdown: Six (with linking consonant ḷ before the vowel).
Having the higher knowledges; from abhiññā.

chaḷabhiñño six supernormal.meaning: chaḷabhiñño: possessing the six supernormal powers
chaḷabhiñño six supernormal.note: uncertain: this headword is a glossary-row artifact; the entry is chaḷabhiñño with the gloss “six supernormal powers”. See chaḷabhiñño and chaḷabhiññā.

chaḷabhiññā.meaning: the six supernormal knowledges; the six psychic powers
chaḷabhiññā.summary: This compound (cha + abhiññā) names the six higher knowledges attained through meditation.
chaḷabhiññā.gram: feminine noun, plural, compound (cha + abhiññā, with linking ḷ)
breakdown: Six (with linking consonant ḷ before the vowel).
Supernatural knowledge, higher power; abhi + √ñā “to know”.
chaḷabhiññā.goenka: The chaḷabhiññā are the six supernatural knowledges or psychic powers: iddhi (magical power such as flying across or through mountains), dibba sota (clairaudience), parassa cetopariyañāṇa (knowing the thoughts of others), pubbe nivāsānussati (recollecting one's previous lives), dibba cakkhu (the divine eye, knowing other beings' rebirths), and āsavakkhaya (the certainty that emancipation has already been attained — the destruction of the mental obsessions). Only the last is uniquely liberating.

chaḷeva.gram: sandhi fusion: cha (with linking ḷ) + eva

chedanavadhabandhanaviparāmosaālopa.meaning: destruction, murder, binding, robbery and plunder
chedanavadhabandhanaviparāmosaālopa.gram: compound (fragment; see the full form ending in -sahasākārā)
chedanavadhabandhanaviparāmosaālopa.note: uncertain: this headword is a truncated form of chedanavadhabandhanaviparāmosaālopasahasākārā; see that entry.

chedanavadhabandhanaviparāmosaālopasahas.meaning: destruction, murder, binding, robbery, plundering and deeds of violence

breakdown: Like, as (iva, shortened and fused in sandhi).

cintasuttaṁ.meaning: the Cinta Sutta, the discourse on thinking (cinta + suttaṁ)

cintīsuttaṁ.meaning: the Cintī Sutta, the discourse of “the thinker” (cintī: having one’s thoughts, thinking)

ciraṭṭhitikā.meaning: long-lasting; enduring for a long time
breakdown: A long time (with sandhi doubling of the following ṭh).

ciraṭṭhitikāmatāya.meaning: out of desire for (its) long endurance
breakdown: Out of desire for; instrumental of kāmatā “desire” (the two members fuse over the shared kā syllable).

cittaklesehi.meaning: from the impurities of the mind; with mental defilements
cittaklesehi.gram: masculine noun, instrumental/ablative plural, compound (citta + klesa)
breakdown: Impurities, stains, defilements; instrumental-ablative plural of klesa (kilesa).

cittasaṇhatāya.meaning: by gentleness of mind; through softness of the mind
cittasaṇhatāya.gram: feminine abstract noun, instrumental singular, compound (citta + saṇha + -tā)
breakdown: Abstract suffix -tā in the instrumental singular.

codeti.meaning: to exhort; to rebuke, reprove; to complain
